---
title: "Week 2 – Portfolio, Xaringan and Quarto"
format: html
---

## Overview

This week focused on the technical setup required for the module portfolio rather than on remote sensing analysis itself. The practical introduced Xaringan for building presentations, Quarto for creating a structured online learning diary, and GitHub Pages for publishing work online.

## What I Learned

One of the most important things I learned this week is that this module is not only about analysing remotely sensed data, but also about how that work is organised, presented, and shared. The idea of using a Quarto book means that the learning diary is cumulative and structured, rather than a set of unrelated weekly notes.

The practical also introduced Xaringan as a way of creating presentations using RMarkdown syntax. This was different from using PowerPoint, because content, layout, and formatting are controlled through code and markdown. Although this seemed more technical at first, it also showed how presentation and reproducibility can be combined.

## GitHub and Workflow

Another key part of this week was understanding the role of Git and GitHub in the module workflow. The process of adding, committing, and pushing work means that progress is recorded over time, which is important because the assessment requires regular weekly updates. This made me realise that organising files and maintaining a clear project structure is an important part of remote sensing practice, not just a background task.

## Reflection

Compared with Week 1, this week felt less focused on image analysis and more focused on infrastructure. At first, setting up Quarto, Xaringan, and GitHub Pages felt technical and slightly overwhelming. However, it became clear that these tools are central to the module because they provide a way to document progress, publish outputs, and connect code with communication.

This week changed how I think about the learning diary. I now understand it not simply as a written reflection, but as part of a digital portfolio that develops over time.

## Future Application

The skills introduced this week will be useful throughout the rest of the module. In particular, Quarto will help structure the diary, GitHub Pages will support online publication, and Xaringan provides a way to communicate ideas more dynamically. These tools seem especially useful for future work where analysis, writing, and presentation need to be combined in one workflow.
